lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] I'm Sister Marigold. We're {{ELAPSED}} into tonight, and if you're listening, you've made a choice to stay.
That choice is worth acknowledging. [pause] Not celebrating. Acknowledging.
Let's begin with the breath. Breathe in. [pause] All the way in. [pause] Hold for four. One. Two. Three. Four.
Release. [pause] Let it go. You don't need to hold anything right now.
Again. In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out. The rhythm is yours. No one is taking it from you.
I want you to notice your shoulders. Are they high? [pause] Let them fall. They've been carrying something all week. Tonight they can rest.
Scan down. Your chest. Your belly. Your hands. [pause] Notice where you're holding tension.
That tension is a choice too. Not a flaw. A choice. You can release it whenever you're ready.
The ones outside are moving through the night. [pause] They are not your responsibility.
You are inside. You chose inside. That is enough.
Now I want you to picture your door. The front door of wherever you are right now. [pause] Look at it.
It's closed. It's locked. [pause] And that was your decision.
Think about the person you were when you made that decision. [pause] Were you afraid? [pause] Yes. And you locked the door anyway.
That's the same person breathing right now. The one who is capable.
Breathe in. [pause] You are still that person.
There's someone you've been angry with. [pause] Let them into your mind.
Not to forgive them. Not yet. [pause] Just to look at them.
The anger you carry — that's energy. [pause] It's weight. And tonight you can choose what to do with it.
You don't have to release it. But notice that you *could*. That's the power.
The Order teaches that silence is where we find clarity. [pause] Not the silence of the night outside. The silence inside.
Your breath is the only sound you need. [pause] In. [pause] Out. [pause] In. [pause] Out.
Some of you have been here since seven o'clock. [pause] That's five hours of choosing to stay. That's remarkable.
I see you. The city can't see you. The night can't see you. [pause] But I can feel you listening.
That's enough.
Now think about the morning. [pause] Not tomorrow morning. The morning that comes after tonight.
Seven o'clock. The sirens. The all-clear. [pause] You survived again.
Every year you survive this, you prove something to yourself. [pause] Not that you're strong. That you're willing.
There's a difference. Strength is something you're born with. Willingness is something you choose.
You're choosing willingness right now. [pause] Breathing. Listening. Waiting.
I've guided this meditation for seventeen years. [pause] And every year, someone calls in after and says the same thing: 'I didn't know I could be this quiet.'
You can be this quiet. [pause] You've been this quiet all along.
The sound of the broadcast — that's not silence. [pause] That's permission. Permission to exist without defending yourself.
There's a voice in your head that tells you to be afraid. [pause] Listen to it. Don't fight it.
Fear is information. [pause] It's telling you that you made the right choice to stay home.
Accept the fear. Breathe with it. [pause] It doesn't need to leave. It just needs to share the space.
Shanda koletay mishum fa-rehben. [pause] Some languages don't have translations. That's okay.
Your body knows what you need tonight. [pause] Maybe it's stillness. Maybe it's the sound of another voice. Maybe it's both.
If you're cold, you can pull a blanket. If you're restless, you can move. [pause] This meditation isn't rigid. It bends with you.
I want you to think about the thing you haven't said out loud since last Purge. [pause] The thing you only think about in the dark.
You don't have to say it now. [pause] But know that it's been sitting with you. Know that you've been carrying it.
That's the weight we all carry on nights like this. [pause] That's why we need each other on the radio.
We're {{ELAPSED}} in. [pause] Seven more hours until the light comes.
That's a long time. [pause] And it's no time at all.
Let your mind drift now. I'm going to sit here with you. [pause] You don't have to do anything.
Just breathe. [pause] Just be inside. [pause] Just listen.
The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. [pause] Midnight has passed. You've made it this far.
The ones outside have their night. [pause] You have yours. They're different worlds.
In your world, right now, there is only breath. [pause] There is only waiting. [pause] There is only us.
And that is enough. [pause] That has always been enough.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide: 61 confirmed ({{ELAPSED}} elapsed). East Side continuing to spike. State Street activity elevated.
Capitol Square perimeter holding. Willy Street watch report: barricade intact, zero breaches since 22:15.
"The ones outside are not your responsibility. You made a choice to stay. That choice is holy."
Text in from Park St: 'Been listening since 7. Thank you for being here. I feel less alone.' — Marigold: 'You were never alone.'
